name: Garm
description: Garm is a monstrous hound associated with Hel and Ragnarök in Norse mythology. Often described as a fearsome wolf or dog, Garm guards the gates of Helheim, the realm of the dead. His baying is a critical sign of the impending doom of Ragnarök, signaling the beginning of the end of the world. Garm is a powerful and terrifying creature, bound with the magical chain Gleipnir, but destined to break free during the final battle. He is locked away in the cave Gnipahellir, where he awaits the day of Ragnarök. He is often considered the most powerful hound in Norse mythology, surpassing even Fenrir in some depictions. Garm's role is primarily as a guardian and harbinger of destruction, a symbol of the chaos and darkness that will engulf the world at the end of days. His ultimate fate is to engage in a fatal battle with the god Týr, resulting in the mutual destruction of both combatants. He is a terrifying representation of the destructive forces that will eventually overcome even the gods themselves. While not as prominently featured as Fenrir, Garm's presence is a constant reminder of the inevitable end.
subtitle: Guardian of Hel, Harbinger of Ragnarök
appearance: Garm is depicted as an enormous, fearsome hound or wolf, often described as being dark in color, sometimes even black. He possesses sharp teeth and claws, and his eyes are often described as glowing or burning with an infernal light. Some accounts portray him with blood-stained jaws, reflecting his role as a guardian of the dead and a harbinger of destruction. His howl is said to be deafening and terrifying, capable of striking fear into the hearts of gods and mortals alike.
habitat: Garm resides primarily in Gnipahellir, a cave located near the gates of Helheim, the realm of the dead ruled by the goddess Hel.
behavior: Garm is primarily a guardian, fiercely protective of the gates of Helheim. He is described as being constantly vigilant, alert to any intrusion. His baying serves as a warning of impending doom and the onset of Ragnarök. He is generally depicted as aggressive and territorial, embodying the destructive and chaotic forces associated with the end of the world. He is loyal to Hel and her realm, serving as her loyal hound and protector.
weaknesses: Fated death at the hands of Týr

keyMyths.title: Ragnarök
keyMyths.description: Garm's baying signals the beginning of Ragnarök. He breaks free from his chains and engages in a fatal battle with the god Týr.

keyMyths.title: Guardian of Helheim
keyMyths.description: Garm guards the gates of Helheim, preventing the dead from escaping and the living from entering without permission. He is a fearsome protector of the underworld.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for Norse mythology, including mentions of Garm and his role in Ragnarök.
sources.text: A key source for Norse mythology, including mentions of Garm and his role in Ragnarök.

sources.author: Various Authors
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems that contain references to Garm and the events of Ragnarök.
